objectives: Identify core blockchain components and how they interact
Explain blockchain architecture at a high level
Describe blockchain openness and its implications for applications
learning_path.title: Blockchain Foundations: Components, Architecture, and Openness

objectives: Describe what constitutes a blockchain application
Map common blockchain application areas to typical requirements
Differentiate application goals that benefit from blockchain openness
learning_path.title: Blockchain Applications and Application Areas

objectives: Explain public-key cryptography concepts used in blockchain systems
Describe how signatures support authentication and authorization
Summarize zk basics and where they may appear in blockchain applications
learning_path.title: Cryptography Basics for Blockchain: Public-Key, Signatures, Zk Basics

objectives: Explain how the Evm executes smart contract code
Describe state, storage, memory, and call semantics at a conceptual level
Relate Evm execution to gas usage and transaction outcomes
learning_path.title: Ethereum Execution Model: Ethereum Virtual Machine (Evm)

objectives: Write basic Solidity contracts with functions, state variables, and events
Apply common Solidity patterns for access control and state updates
Connect Solidity constructs to Evm execution behavior
learning_path.title: Solidity Fundamentals: Writing Smart Contracts

objectives: Explain the purpose and core interfaces of Erc-20, Erc-721, and Erc-1155
Implement minimal compliant contracts in Solidity
Compare tradeoffs and appropriate use cases across token standards
learning_path.title: Token Standards: Erc-20, Erc-721, Erc-1155

objectives: Set up a Node.Js-based project structure for blockchain development
Manage scripts and configuration commonly used in contract workflows
Prepare a Node.Js environment for interacting with deployed contracts
learning_path.title: Node.Js for Blockchain Development Workflows

objectives: Connect to a blockchain node and read on-chain data
Send transactions and handle receipts and errors
Interact with token contracts using standard interfaces
learning_path.title: DApp Interaction: Web3.Js/Ethers.Js

objectives: Create automated test suites for Solidity contracts
Test token standard behaviors and edge cases
Use local execution to validate expected state transitions
learning_path.title: Smart Contract Testing Frameworks: Foundry/Hardhat/Truffle

objectives: Identify common smart contract vulnerability classes
Apply secure design and coding practices in Solidity
Review contracts for security issues using structured checklists
learning_path.title: Smart Contract Security: Threats, Vulnerabilities, and Mitigations

objectives: Apply security principles to end-to-end blockchain applications
Analyze trust boundaries across on-chain and off-chain components
Design safer transaction flows and key usage patterns
learning_path.title: Blockchain Applications Security Principles

objectives: Explain how gas costs arise from Evm operations and storage patterns
Refactor Solidity code to reduce gas usage while preserving behavior
Validate gas improvements through testing workflows
learning_path.title: Gas Optimization: Efficient Smart Contract Design

matched_occupation.description: Blockchain developers implement or program blockchain-based software systems based on specifications and designs by using programming languages, tools, and blockchain platforms.
matched_occupation.label: blockchain developer
